text:
Market today: Tech/chips are the story today. Nomura is calling out overlooked bottlenecks and hyperscaler spending as reasons the chip rally has more legs. That's a direct tailwind for your heaviest exposure - semiconductors and mega-cap tech. Mood is constructive but not euphoric; we're in premium territory on most names, so the setup rewards holders more than chasers right now.

Your holdings: All positions are on HOLD and in the green - nothing screaming danger today. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged ETF and your stop at $200 is wide; on margin, a fast reversal would hit hard - worth keeping that stop front of mind. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile - it's a leveraged ETF, moves fast, decays, and you're barely in the money (+1.91%). Margin amplifies any drop here. Everything else looks fine today.

Watch: 1) NVDA reaction to any hyperscaler/AI spending commentary - it's your sector's bellwether. 2) Whether chip names can hold premium levels after the Nomura call; a fade would be the first warning.

Bottom line: You're positioned right for today's tape - protect gains on the leveraged names and let the rest run.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at $200 is set - honor it without hesitation. On 3x leverage plus margin, a quick reversal compounds fast; no reason to widen it just because today is green.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, barely off entry, on margin - keep your stop at $21.38 firm. If it doesn't build momentum soon, it's a candidate to cut first if the market softens.
NVDA: No new buy today - scanner rates it 7/12 (Grade C, 4H down) with a tiny R of 1.4. Nomura tailwind is real but pattern is weak; watch for a cleaner setup before adding exposure.
